title: Socket communication with an IP *.*.*.1 fails
body:
### Issue Description

Socket communications cannot be achieved with an IP in *.*.*.1 in a created network ("connection refused").

(Hypothesis, cf how to reproduce) Podman allows to run a container with IP *.*.*.1 while it's already in use.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman network create tmp --subnet 10.0.0.0/16`
2. `podman run --network tmp:ip=10.0.0.1 -it alpine`
3. `podman run --network tmp:ip=10.0.0.2 -it alpine`
4. Try to make them communicate with sockets (I can add a minimalist example in go if needed)

### Describe the results you received

The container is running with the requested IP but cannot dial with another (e.g. *.*.*.2) while containers in *.*.*.2 and *.*.*.[3-254] can communicate whithout any issue.

### Describe the results you expected

If my hypothesis is right, a similar result than what we get with docker (i.e.: raise an error):

`docker: Error response from daemon: failed to set up container networking: Address already in use`

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

It may be related but with podman it is possible to ping every address that have not yet been assigned to a container while it's not possible with docker.

comments.body:
> podman run --network tmp:10.0.0.1 -it

That is not valid syntax to begin with, setting a static ips needs `tmp:ip=10.0.0.1` .

But besides if you do not give a gateway ip it will default to the .1 address of the subnet so that address must not be used for containers again as it results in a address conflict.
So yes it seems our logic does not reject gateway assignments which is a bug.
Hi @Chaman-veteran  and @Luap99.  I'd like to work on this issue. I'll investigate and submit a PR if I can get it working.

@Luap99 yeah for the syntax, my bad, I wrote it too fast :p (and edited my message)

@kavirakesh14 thanks for taking this issue! I wanted to dig into this but as I am not aligned with this project's stance on LLM, I quickly avorted this idea

relevant code paths are in another repo
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/container-libs/blob/main/common/libnetwork/netavark/ipam.go

Basically just need to also check the gateway against the static ip assignment.
Hi @Luap99 and @Chaman-veteran , I've opened a PR with the fix over at container-libs#894
